content:
ELDERCRAFT® BLACK ELDERBERRY: THE NATURAL REMEDY FOR SINUS, COLD & FLU

What is Black Elderberry?

Black Elderberry is known as European Elderberry (Sambucus nigra L.). It is common in hedgerows in Ireland and England and cultivated for commercial use throughout Europe.

Although There are Sambucas species in the U.S. and throughout North America, such as American Elderberry (Sambucus canadensis) and Blue Elderberry (Sambucus cerulea), but they are obviously not the same as Black Elderberry (Sambucus nigra L.).

Black Elderberry has been used medically for centuries. It has been used in folk medicine to treat influenza, colds and sinusitis, and has been reported to have antiviral activity against influenza, colds and sinusitis, and has been reported to have antiviral activity against influenza. In 400 BCE, Hippocreates, the father of medicine, referred to the elderberry tree as his "medicine chest". In the early 20 century, British herbalist, Maud Grieves, provided a comprehensive review of the historical uses of elderberry as a traditional medicine. Prior to antibiotics, elderberry was found as one of the main ingredients in many preparations used by herbalists, pharmacists, and physicians. Today, elderberry is employed as an alternative to conventional medicines and mainly in the form of extract for treating the common cold, influenza and Herpes virus infections.

ElderCraft® is a clinically proven black elderberry extract from Iprona AG, Italy.

Benefits of Eldercraft® Black Elderberries:

Colds/Flu:

Eldercraft® Black Elderberries are most famously used for the treatment of colds and flu. There is much evidence for its effectiveness.

Human clinical trial by Associate Professor Evelin Tiralongo and Dr. Shirley Wee of the School of Pharmacy, Griffith University, Australia. The study involved 312 economy class passengers traveling from Australia to an overseas destination. As well as jet lag and fatigue, passengers may experience upper respiratory symptoms as a result of international flights.

This two year randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial was done using IPRONA's Eldercraft® standardized elderberry extract. Results of this large, human clinical trial reveal that IPRONA's Eldercraft® proprietary elderberry formula can provide protection to air travellers from colds as well as stabilize overall health during long-haul flights.

Study tested if 600-900mg of Iprona AG's standardized membrane enriched Elderberry Formula have beneficial effects on physical health, especially respiratory health.

The team included 312 long haul flight passengers, over a two year period. 158 passengers received Elderberry capsules for 10 days prior to and 5 days after flight.

Iprona AG Tested Result:
Iprona AG's standardized membrane enriched Elderberry Formula has proven effective against flu virus and bacteria that can cause upper respiratory infections.

Average cold duration: 5 days (elderberry group) vs 7 days (control group)

Fun Numbers:
- Coughing releases 3000 droplets at speeds of up to 20mph
- Sneezing releases droplets at speeds of up to 100mph
- Flu virus can survive on hard surfaces for 24 hours
- Droplets can spread up to 50 feet from infected individuals

Sinus Infection:
The Elderflower is an anti-catarrhal herb, meaning it is extremely effective for runny noses and congestion. Anti-catarrhal herbs prevent excess mucous formation and aid in removing mucous and reducing inflammation in the body.

Immune System:

Black Elderberries are particularly rich in flavonoids. These powerful antioxidants work to keep the immune system strong and resilient. The President of Germany's Federal Research Institute for Nutrition and Food, Dr Gerhard Rech-kemmer's research has shown that the anthocyanins in elderberries boost the production of cytokines. Cytokines play a crucial role in the immune system's response to disease and work in ways very similar to hormones.

These berries are also believed to contain antiviral agents—compounds so potent they are thought to deactivate viruses. Viruses are unable to multiply on their own and need to get inside a healthy cell to do so. They are cleverly coated with something called "haemagglutinin spikes" to help them pierce the cell wall. These viral spikes are also covered with enzymes which the virus uses to break down the cell wall. Black Elderberries have high concentrations of bioflavonoids which appear to inhibit the action of this enzyme, thus deactivating viruses and rendering them unable to pierce the cell wall and replicate.

The Elderflower is also packed with bio-flavonoids that help to boost the immune system. Research has shown that Elderflowers are also effective in killing common pathogens.

Allergy:

Black Elderberry's immunostimulating and anti-inflammatory properties make it a great natural remedy for allergy symptoms. Inflammation of the sinuses, sneezing, itchy and watery eyes, swelling and a runny or stuffy nose can all benefit from this potent herb. It is thought to be particularly good for hayfever and strengthening the upper respiratory tract.

Digestive System:

The high fibre content of black elderberries can help to eliminate constipation, reduce excess gas and generally increase the health of the gastrointestinal system. The dietary fibre contained in these berries can also increase nutrient uptake efficiency, helping you to get more nutrients out of the food that you eat.
